description: Raina confronts Gordon in Afterlife, expressing her desire to leave and frustration at her situation. Gordon tries to comfort her, promising that she will eventually see the gift in her transformation and pledging to guide her. Their exchange touches on themes of acceptance, guidance, and the difficulty of change.

transcript.line: Am I free to go? Skye had a choice. Are you telling me I can't leave?
I can take you to beautiful places, pyramids by moonlight, an island untouched by man. But it is too dangerous in public right now.
You don't know that. I'm a survivor.
I know you are. And you're too good to pity yourself. I know isolation, pain. Think this face didn't take some getting used to?
But first, I was blind, lost in the world. These gifts are deeply layered for people like us. Now I can go anywhere find our people.
Well, what was the good in finding me? If I have a use, I don't know it.
Yet. I will be your guide. Your beauty will be reflected in a wonderful gift. People like us have to live differently, but it doesn't mean it can't be an amazing world.
As long as I have you to show it to me.
